code:
import datetime

class User:
    def __init__(self, username, email, id=None, is_active=True, created_at=None, last_login=None):
        self.id = id
        self.username = username
        self.email = email
        self.is_active = is_active
        # If created_at is not provided, set it to the current UTC time.
        # Using UTC is a good practice for timestamps.
        self.created_at = created_at if created_at is not None else datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc)
        self.last_login = last_login

    def __repr__(self):
        return (f"User(id={self.id}, "
                f"username={self.username!r}, "
                f"email={self.email!r}, "
                f"is_active={self.is_active}, "
                f"created_at={self.created_at!r}, "
                f"last_login={self.last_login!r})")
